title: Benchling Launches Three Products During SynBioBeta 2026 — and Its Head of AI Hints at What Comes Next
summary: Benchling launched three products at SynBioBeta 2026: One-Click Ordering (integrating design-to-order workflow with Twist Bioscience, Adaptyv, and Ginkgo Bioworks to close the gap between computational design and physical synthesis), Benchling Biologics (an end-to-end antibody R&D platform automating entity configuration and validation at scale), and a Data Analysis skill for its AI platform powered by Nicholas Larus-Stone. Larus-Stone, Benchling's newly named Head of AI, predicted that 75% of biological data analysis tasks will be handled by AI agents within a year—a vision immediately followed by the shipping of the corresponding tool.
analysis_notes: Article from Aaron Blotnick dated May 12, 2026. Strong signal article: Benchling (a major AI platform player in bio R&D) shipped all three products rapidly and backlogged the headline claim with concrete capability launches. High priority given Benchling's stated 1,300+ customer base and direct competition space for AI-augmented lab software. One minor note: URL in share links uses example.com; likely placeholder from SynBioBeta press site but does not affect article substance.
